cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what made Florence Nightingale decide to become a nurse. First, I need to go back to the text and find the relevant part. The passage says, "As a child she felt that visiting sick people was both a duty and a pleasure. She enjoyed helping them. At last mind was made up. 'I'm going to be a nurse,' she decided." So, the key sentence is that she felt visiting sick people was a duty and a pleasure, so she wanted to help them. That led her to decide to become a nurse. Looking at the choices: (B) Her desire to help the sick. That matches with what the text says. Other options: (A) Her father's support. Actually, the father says nursing isn't right for a lady, so he didn't support her. (C) Her education in Germany and France—she went there to learn nursing after deciding to be a nurse, not
